decisions.summary: Node.js 20+ and Python 3.11+ are the Gate A runtimes.
npm with package-lock.json is the JavaScript package manager.
The ordinary command surface is init, sync, status, restore.
Gate A code is limited to help/version, discovery, validation, and contract verification.
docs/architecture sources are immutable manifest-verified inputs.
schemas/ and examples/ are non-authoritative working mirrors.
Identifiers are opaque and prefix-scoped.
Relative paths are normalized, UTF-8, safe, and forward-slash based.
SHA-256 is the mandatory digest algorithm.
Tree-content digests hash canonical UTF-8 JSON for sorted complete tree content.
Material, semantic, workflow, and projection records remain separate families.
Authority descriptors require immutable registration decisions before acceptance.
Assertions and decisions are distinct immutable records.
Deterministic and model-assisted projections are separate attributable outputs.
Python hooks are schema-governed read-only stdin/stdout participants.
Adapter inspection, cursors, idempotency, reconciliation, policy, and writer partitioning are deferred outside core.
Core extension exports are narrow and documented.
Gate A contains no locks, stores, transactions, recovery, or receipts.
Source-content security is governance and test contract until later runtime phases.
Gate A closure requires decisions, schemas, registries, fixtures, generated types, and validator parity to agree.
